records.title: Choose and register with a GP
records.description: Residents are strongly advised to register with a nearby general practitioner before care is needed and provide identification, BSN, insurance details, medication information and relevant medical records.
records.ai_summary: Find a practice serving the home address and ask whether it accepts new patients. Register before an urgent problem arises and transfer current medication and medical-history information securely.

records.title: Contact the GP practice and arrange care
records.description: A GP practice can offer telephone advice, appointments, e-consultations, repeat prescriptions, home visits and urgent assessment, with the assistant triaging the most suitable response.
records.ai_summary: Describe symptoms and urgency clearly to the assistant and follow the offered care route. Contact the practice during opening hours for routine issues and its urgent line for problems that cannot wait.

records.title: Who works in a GP practice
records.description: Dutch GP practices use multidisciplinary teams including assistants, practice nurses, mental-health support staff and physician assistants, each working within defined responsibilities under GP oversight.

official_source.title: Met wie krijg je te maken bij de huisartsenpraktijk?

records.ai_summary: The first contact may be with an assistant or another qualified team member rather than the GP. Ask who is responsible for the consultation and how follow-up or referral will be arranged.

records.title: GP care in the basic insurance package
records.description: Care delivered by a GP and the practice team is covered by the statutory basic package, while related tests, medicines or referred care may have different deductible and policy conditions.
records.ai_summary: GP consultations themselves are part of the basic package and are exempt from the compulsory deductible. Check separately how laboratory work, medicines and follow-on treatment are charged.

records.title: Mental-health help through the GP
records.description: A GP and general-practice mental-health worker can assess and treat mild problems and arrange referral to more specialised mental healthcare when symptoms are complex or severe.

official_source.title: Where can I get help for mental health problems?

records.ai_summary: Discuss mental-health symptoms with the GP or practice support worker. They can provide initial care, assess safety and create a referral when treatment by a psychologist or psychiatrist is indicated.

records.title: Midwifery care and pregnancy
records.description: Midwifery care during pregnancy and birth is included in the basic package, while provider choice, place of birth and possible personal contributions depend on medical need and policy conditions.
records.ai_summary: Contact a midwifery practice early in pregnancy and check the insurer's provider conditions. Hospital birth without a medical indication can have a different personal contribution from medically indicated care.

records.title: Prescription medicines and pharmacy costs
records.description: The basic package covers eligible medicines under statutory and policy conditions, while reimbursement limits, preferred-medicine policies, pharmacy service fees and the deductible can affect personal costs.
records.ai_summary: Ask the prescriber and pharmacy whether the medicine is covered, whether a preferred equivalent applies and whether a personal contribution or deductible will be charged. Keep an updated medication list.

records.title: Dental care and basic insurance
records.description: Basic dental coverage differs strongly by age and type of treatment, with broader routine coverage for children and limited statutory coverage for most adult dental care.
records.ai_summary: Check age-specific basic coverage and any supplementary policy before treatment. Ask the dental provider for an estimate when substantial work is planned and confirm which items remain payable personally.

records.title: Physiotherapy and exercise therapy coverage
records.description: Basic-package reimbursement for physiotherapy depends on age, diagnosis and treatment count, with different rules for listed chronic conditions and several specifically covered programmes.
records.ai_summary: Confirm whether the diagnosis and planned treatment series meet basic-package conditions and whether the provider is contracted. Otherwise check supplementary insurance or the expected self-pay amount before starting.

records.title: District nursing at home
records.description: Adults who need nursing or personal care in a medical context can contact a district-nursing provider directly for assessment, generally without a GP referral or compulsory deductible.
records.ai_summary: Contact a district-nursing organisation for an assessment of care needs. Verify insurer authorisation or contracting conditions and whether the need falls under health insurance, municipal support or long-term care.
